reasoning: To reach the resolution threshold, Argentina would need over 1,239 distinct ACLED 'Protest' events within a single 30-day period (10x the historical 360-day baseline of ~124 per month). This equates to over 41 protests per day. Since the start of President Milei's administration (16 months), Argentina has seen just over 2,000 total protests (~125 per month on average). Even during intense months of unrest—such as February 2025, which saw double the previous month's protests (155 events), and the April 2025 general strike—the volume falls short of the threshold by a factor of eight. Sustaining 41+ distinct protest events daily for a full month would require a total breakdown of civil order unseen in Argentina's recent past, and highly unprecedented for ACLED reporting anywhere in South America without cascading systemic crisis. While general strikes cause spikes, they don't produce the raw count of 1240 distinct geographic/temporal protest events. Given constraints on minimum probability (0.05) and increasing uncertainty over time horizons, I expect this to firmly resolve NO across all dates.
